name: agent-sdk
context: Build autonomous Claude agents with the Claude Agent SDK — tools, hooks, MCP servers, and multi-agent orchestration
info: The Claude Agent SDK (`pip install claude-agent-sdk`) provides a Python framework for building autonomous agents powered by Claude Code. The core primitives are `query()` for simple one-shot prompts, `ClaudeSDKClient` for interactive stateful sessions with custom tools and hooks, and the `@tool` decorator for defining in-process MCP server tools. Agents can read, write, and edit files, run bash commands, and use any MCP server. Configuration via `ClaudeAgentOptions` controls: system prompt, allowed/disallowed tools, permission mode (askUser, acceptEdits, denyEdits), max turns, working directory, and MCP server connections. Hooks enable deterministic interception at PreToolUse and PostToolUse points for guardrails. The SDK supports both in-process MCP tools (no subprocess, better performance) and external MCP servers via stdio.
triggers: create an autonomous agent with claude
define tools for a claude agent
add hooks to a claude agent
build a multi-agent system with claude
body:
# Claude Agent SDK

Build autonomous agents that use Claude Code's full toolset: file I/O, bash, code editing, and custom MCP tools.

## Installation

```bash
pip install claude-agent-sdk
```

Requires Python 3.10+. Claude Code CLI is bundled automatically.

## Quick Start — Simple Query

```python
import anyio
from claude_agent_sdk import query, AssistantMessage, TextBlock

async def main():
    async for message in query(prompt="What is 2 + 2?"):
        if isinstance(message, AssistantMessage):
            for block in message.content:
                if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
                    print(block.text)

anyio.run(main)
```

## Interactive Client with Options

```python
from claude_agent_sdk import ClaudeSDKClient, ClaudeAgentOptions, AssistantMessage, TextBlock

options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
    system_prompt="You are a senior Python developer. Write clean, tested code.",
    allowed_tools=["Read", "Write", "Edit", "Bash"],
    permission_mode="acceptEdits",
    max_turns=5,
    cwd="/path/to/project",
)

async with ClaudeSDKClient(options=options) as client:
    await client.query("Create a hello.py file that prints 'Hello, World!'")
    async for msg in client.receive_response():
        if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
            for block in msg.content:
                if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
                    print(block.text)
```

## ClaudeAgentOptions

| Option | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `system_prompt` | str | Custom system prompt |
| `max_turns` | int | Max conversation turns |
| `cwd` | str/Path | Working directory |
| `allowed_tools` | list[str] | Auto-approve these tools |
| `disallowed_tools` | list[str] | Block these tools |
| `permission_mode` | str | `askUser`, `acceptEdits`, `denyEdits` |
| `mcp_servers` | dict | MCP server configurations |
| `hooks` | dict | Hook matchers for interception |

## Custom Tools via @tool Decorator

Define in-process MCP tools that run without subprocess overhead:

```python
from claude_agent_sdk import tool, create_sdk_mcp_server, ClaudeSDKClient, ClaudeAgentOptions

@tool("lookup_user", "Look up a user by email address", {"email": str})
async def lookup_user(args):
    email = args["email"]
    # Your actual user lookup logic
    return {
        "content": [{"type": "text", "text": f"User: {email}, Status: active, Plan: pro"}]
    }

@tool("send_email", "Send an email to a user", {"to": str, "subject": str, "body": str})
async def send_email(args):
    # Your actual email sending logic
    return {
        "content": [{"type": "text", "text": f"Email sent to {args['to']}"}]
    }

# Create MCP server with tools
server = create_sdk_mcp_server(
    name="user-tools",
    version="1.0.0",
    tools=[lookup_user, send_email]
)

# Use with client
options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
    system_prompt="You are a customer support agent.",
    mcp_servers={"users": server},
    allowed_tools=["mcp__users__lookup_user", "mcp__users__send_email"],
)

async with ClaudeSDKClient(options=options) as client:
    await client.query("Look up user alice@example.com and send them a welcome email")
    async for msg in client.receive_response():
        if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
            for block in msg.content:
                if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
                    print(block.text)
```

## Hooks (Guardrails)

Intercept tool calls for safety and deterministic processing:

```python
from claude_agent_sdk import ClaudeSDKClient, ClaudeAgentOptions, HookMatcher

async def block_dangerous_commands(input_data, tool_use_id, context):
    """Deny bash commands that contain dangerous patterns."""
    if input_data["tool_name"] != "Bash":
        return {}

    command = input_data["tool_input"].get("command", "")
    dangerous = ["rm -rf", "sudo", "chmod 777", "dd if="]

    for pattern in dangerous:
        if pattern in command:
            return {
                "hookSpecificOutput": {
                    "hookEventName": "PreToolUse",
                    "permissionDecision": "deny",
                    "permissionDecisionReason": f"Blocked: contains '{pattern}'"
                }
            }
    return {}

options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
    allowed_tools=["Bash", "Read", "Write"],
    hooks={
        "PreToolUse": [
            HookMatcher(matcher="Bash", hooks=[block_dangerous_commands])
        ]
    }
)
```

## External MCP Servers

```python
options = ClaudeAgentOptions(
    mcp_servers={
        "internal": server,  # In-process (from create_sdk_mcp_server)
        "database": {
            "type": "stdio",
            "command": "npx",
            "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-postgres", "postgresql://localhost/mydb"]
        }
    }
)
```

## Available Built-in Tools

| Tool | Purpose |
|------|----------|
| `Read` | Read files |
| `Write` | Create files |
| `Edit` | Modify files |
| `Bash` | Run shell commands |
| `Glob` | Find files by pattern |
| `Grep` | Search file contents |

## Message Types

```python
from claude_agent_sdk import (
    AssistantMessage,
    UserMessage,
    SystemMessage,
    ResultMessage,
    TextBlock,
    ToolUseBlock,
    ToolResultBlock,
)

async for message in query(prompt="Hello"):
    if isinstance(message, AssistantMessage):
        for block in message.content:
            if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
                print("Text:", block.text)
            elif isinstance(block, ToolUseBlock):
                print("Tool:", block.name, block.input)
```

## Error Handling

```python
from claude_agent_sdk import (
    ClaudeSDKError,
    CLINotFoundError,
    CLIConnectionError,
    ProcessError,
)

try:
    async for msg in query(prompt="Hello"):
        pass
except CLINotFoundError:
    print("Install Claude Code: curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash")
except ProcessError as e:
    print(f"Process error (exit {e.exit_code})")
```

sections.title: Quick start
sections.body: Install with `pip install claude-agent-sdk` (Python 3.10+). Use `query()` for simple one-shot prompts. Use `ClaudeSDKClient` for interactive sessions with custom tools. Configure via `ClaudeAgentOptions` with system prompt, allowed tools, permission mode, and MCP servers.

sections.title: Custom tools via MCP
sections.body: Define tools with the `@tool(name, description, schema)` decorator. Create an in-process MCP server with `create_sdk_mcp_server(name, version, tools)`. Pass to `ClaudeAgentOptions(mcp_servers={...})`. Auto-approve with `allowed_tools=["mcp__servername__toolname"]`. In-process tools avoid subprocess overhead and are easier to debug than external MCP servers.

sections.title: Hooks for guardrails
sections.body: Hooks intercept agent actions at PreToolUse and PostToolUse. Define async functions that receive `input_data`, `tool_use_id`, and `context`. Return `permissionDecision: 'deny'` to block dangerous operations. Use `HookMatcher(matcher, hooks)` to target specific tools. Hooks enable deterministic safety checks in an otherwise autonomous agent loop.

sections.title: Permission modes
sections.body: Three permission modes: `askUser` (prompt for every tool use), `acceptEdits` (auto-approve file edits), `denyEdits` (deny file modifications). Use `allowed_tools` to auto-approve specific tools regardless of mode. Use `disallowed_tools` to block specific tools. Combine with hooks for fine-grained control.
